mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar XK 4.0 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned XK 4.0 engine typically costs £3,000–£6,000 supply only depending on variant. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on location and workshop rates. A full rebuild including all bearings, pistons, valves, springs and timing gear costs approximately £5,000 (£3,000 in parts, £2,000 in labour excl. VAT). Main dealer new engines are no longer available. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar XK 4.0 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 4.0 AJ-V8 is a solid engine with a good reputation if properly maintained. However, it has two well-documented weaknesses: (1) plastic timing chain tensioners that can fail and destroy the engine, and (2) Nikasil cylinder liners on pre-1999 engines that could fail due to high sulphur fuel. Upgrading to metal tensioners and confirming Nikasil health are essential. The 4.2-litre engine introduced in 2003 resolved both issues.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar XK 4.0?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Plastic timing chain tensioner failure — the most critical weakness, causing cold-start rattle and risk of catastrophic engine damage, (2) Nikasil cylinder liner failure on pre-1999 engines — causing loss of compression, (3) Cooling system failures — plastic water pump, crossover pipe and thermostat housing, and (4) Supercharger coupler wear on XKR models.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar XK 4.0 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £6,000–£15,000 XK, a £3,000–£5,000 recon is 20–80% of the car's value. For a clean example with good body and no rust, replacement is often worth it given the XK's rising classic status. However, if the car has other issues (convertible top, transmission, rust), evaluate total restoration costs before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar XK 4.0 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The XK 4.0 uses the AJ26 (naturally aspirated) or AJ27S (supercharged) engine codes. The XK8 has the naturally aspirated 4.0 (AJ26); the XKR has the supercharged 4.0 (AJ27S). The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I replace the plastic timing chain tensioners, or does it need engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the plastic tensioners can be replaced with upgraded metal tensioners without removing the engine. A preventative replacement of the upper tensioners costs under £250 as a DIY. This is the single most important maintenance item on any 4.0 XK. At the first sign of rattling, book an inspection immediately. If the tensioner fails and the chain snaps, a full engine replacement is usually the only option.

eraMap.title: Jaguar XK 4.0 — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: 4.0L naturally aspirated V8 — 284–294 hp. Nikasil liners (pre-1999) and plastic tensioners are the key risks
4.0L supercharged V8 — 360–370 hp. Same block; plastic tensioners and Eaton M112 supercharger are weak points

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an XK 4.0 engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). Timing chain tensioner replacement is a critical preventative job costing £250–£500 in parts and labour.

cta.label: → Compare XK 4.0 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Plastic Timing Chain Tensioner Failure (The Achilles Heel)
problems.symptoms: Rattling or ticking noise from the engine on cold start, especially from the top of the engine; metallic noise that subsides as oil pressure builds; sudden engine failure if tensioner breaks
problems.repairCost: £250–£500 (preventative upper tensioner replacement — DIY possible) / £1,500–£3,000 (full timing chain kit)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The plastic-bodied upper timing chain tensioners are the "major achilles heel" of the 4.0 AJ-V8. Pre-2003 engines have four timing chains and four plastic tensioners that can break up over time. If a tensioner fails, the chain can snap and destroy the engine. The top "secondary" tensioners (which tension the short chain between the two camshafts) are the most at risk. A preventative replacement of the upper tensioners costs under £250 as a DIY using an upgraded metal tensioner kit. At the first sign of rattling, get the tensioners inspected immediately — this is the single most important maintenance item on any 4.0 XK.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain tensioner guide → /failures/aj-v8-timing-chain-tensioners/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Nikasil Cylinder Liner Failure (Pre-1999 Engines)
problems.symptoms: Loss of compression, rough running, misfires, increased oil consumption, hard starting, failed emissions test
problems.typicalMileage: 40,000–100,000 miles — affected by high sulphur fuel

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: have a compression test performed; if low readings, book inspection urgently

problems.recommendation: Early AJ26 engines (pre-1999) used Nikasil-coated cylinder bores. In the late 1990s, low-quality fuel with high sulphur content caused the Nikasil lining to erode, leading to loss of compression and engine failure. By the end of 1999, Jaguar replaced Nikasil with more reliable cast-iron sleeves. If you're considering a pre-1999 XK, have a compression test performed. Most Nikasil engines that are still running today have survived the issue, but it's worth confirming with a specialist.

failureLink.label: Read the full Nikasil failure guide → /failures/nikasil-cylinder-liner-failure/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Cooling System Failures (Water Pump & Crossover Pipe)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, overheating, steam from engine bay, temperature gauge rising, heater blowing cold
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — plastic components age and fail
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if temperature rises

problems.recommendation: The 4.0 V8 has a "plastic water pump" as a known failure point. The coolant crossover pipe is another common failure, along with ageing hoses and the thermostat housing. Cooling system failures are a "primary weakness" that can cause "premature engine failure if neglected". Preventative replacement of the water pump, crossover pipe and thermostat at 60,000–80,000 miles is strongly recommended. If the temperature gauge rises, shut the engine down immediately — overheating an aluminium V8 can cause catastrophic damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full cooling system failure guide

problems.title: Supercharger Coupler Wear (XKR, AJ27S)
problems.symptoms: Rattling or clattering noise from the supercharger at idle, reduced boost, loss of power, poor throttle response
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — the Eaton M112 coupler wears over time
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)
problems.recommendation: The supercharged AJ27S engine in the XKR uses an Eaton M112 supercharger. The supercharger coupler — a small plastic/nylon component that connects the supercharger rotor to the drive pulley — can wear over time, causing a rattling noise at idle. If you hear rattling from the supercharger, have the coupler inspected. Replacement is a straightforward specialist job that restores full supercharger performance.

failureLink.label: Read the full supercharger coupler guide → /failures/supercharger-coupler-wear/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

buyingChecks.title: Buying This XK 4.0? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-1999 engine with cast-iron liners (Nikasil issue resolved)
* Documented timing chain tensioner replacement with upgraded metal parts — this is the critical item
* Full service history with regular oil changes
* Cooling system overhaul completed (water pump, crossover pipe, thermostat)
avoidIf: * Pre-1999 engine with no Nikasil test or documentation
* Rattling or ticking from the top of the engine — tensioner warning
* Overheating history or cooling system issues — the aluminium V8 is unforgiving of overheating
* Poor service history — the 4.0 V8 is a solid engine but needs proper maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Jaguar XK 4.0's AJ-V8 is a landmark engine — Jaguar's first production V8. It delivers a glorious soundtrack and effortless performance, with up to 294 hp in naturally aspirated form and 370 hp in the supercharged XKR. The engine has a solid reputation if properly maintained, but the plastic timing chain tensioners are the critical weak point — they must be replaced with upgraded metal versions as a matter of urgency. Pre-1999 Nikasil liners are also a concern. Buy a well-maintained example with documented tensioner work and cooling system history. Without history? The repair bills can be substantial.

cta.label: → Compare XK 4.0 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar XK 4.0 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or ticking from the top of the engine (tensioner warning — this is critical)
* Tensioner History — Have the plastic tensioners been replaced with upgraded metal parts? This is the single most important question
* Nikasil Check — Pre-1999 cars need a compression test to confirm liner health
* Service History — Including the AJ26/AJ27S engine and confirmed oil change intervals
* Cooling System — Check for coolant leaks; ask about water pump, crossover pipe and thermostat history
* Supercharger Noise — Any rattling at idle? (XKR models — coupler wear)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for misfire or timing-related codes
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

engineCodes.matchNote: This table's rows match Section 1B's Era Map exactly.

items.label: * Cylinder Block
items.value: Aluminium V8 (Nikasil pre-1999 / cast-iron sleeves post-1999)
